summary: A multi-story residential or institutional building with a distinctive gable roof. The structure features a rectangular footprint with cream/tan colored walls divided by horizontal bands, brick gable roof, and a white fence foundation. Multiple small windows are arranged in regular rows across the facade. The building appears to be 3-4 stories tall with symmetrical design.
elements: gable roof with stepped brick pattern
rebuild_hints: Build walls 4 blocks high using sandstone or similar tan material
Add horizontal bands of darker material (stonebrick) between floors
Place small square windows in regular 2-block spacing across all wall faces
Build gable roof using brick with stepped/pyramidal peak pattern
Add white fence blocks around entire perimeter at ground level
Ensure bilateral symmetry on all sides
Include stone slab trim details at roof edges and transitions
uncertainties: Exact window material (appears to be dark, possibly dark oak or similar)
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Precise block count for roof peak stepping pattern
Whether horizontal bands are full blocks or partial slabs
Exact height may be 13-15 blocks depending on slab usage
Door/entrance location not clearly visible in provided views
